tests.title: LIVE-COMBAT: Tier-2 reflex fire chaff - fires only in danger, refused when safe, allowlist, abort (issue #36)
tests.requires: SAFETY-CRITICAL / LIVE-COMBAT. `[reflex].enabled = true`, `[reflex].allowlist = ["chaff"]`, keep `[reflex].combat_guard = true`, bind your chaff launcher to a KEY in ED. Requires `[elite].enabled`. Chaff is purely DEFENSIVE (never shoots anyone) - the 'fires' cases are safe, but test only against WEAK NPCs.
tests.expected: Startup reports the chaff bind + reflexes-ON line; saying 'chaff' while safe is refused (proving the guard without a fight); with ED off it's refused; under fire from a weak NPC it presses chaff once; without chaff allowlisted it's neither advertised nor run; 'abort' releases all held keys; Tier-1 landing gear still refuses in combat (independent policies); disabling removes the chaff tool.

steps.text: Startup readiness: launch reports `Reflex: chaff -> <key>` (or `chaff UNUSABLE (no keyboard bind for FireChaffLauncher)`), and a `Tier-2 combat reflexes ON ...` line. (Add heat_sink to the allowlist to also see it and say 'heat sink!' under fire - same guard.)

Refused when safe (fully combat-SAFE - do this parked/docked): say 'chaff' while NOT in danger -> COVAS refuses ('you're not in combat...') and nothing is pressed. This is the safe way to prove the guard without a fight.
Refused with monitoring off: set `[elite].enabled = false`, say 'chaff' -> refused ('can't confirm you're in danger...'); nothing fires.

Fires under fire (defensive - safe): let a weak NPC interdict you (or take fire from a low-threat hostile), say 'chaff' -> it presses your chaff key once, log `fired chaff -> <key>`, and chaff deploys.
Not offered unless allowlisted: remove chaff from `[reflex].allowlist` (leave enabled), ask for chaff -> it's neither advertised nor run.
Hard abort: say 'abort' -> releases every held key (shared with keybinds/honk). Log: `aborted - released all keys`.
Tier-1 unaffected: with reflexes on, confirm 6.1 landing gear still refuses in combat (the two policies are independent).
Disabled: set `[reflex].enabled = false` -> no chaff tool offered; asking for chaff does nothing.

tests.title: LIVE-COMBAT: Tier-2 reflex fast path - second-PTT phrase-spotter, no LLM, same guard (issue #38)
tests.requires: SAFETY-CRITICAL / LIVE-COMBAT. `[reflex].enabled = true`, `[reflex].allowlist = ["chaff"]`, bind `[reflex].ptt` to a DIFFERENT key than `[keys].push_to_talk` (e.g. `]` or a spare HOTAS button via JoyToKey). Requires `[elite].enabled`. A local hotword path - matches a fixed vocabulary and fires without the LLM, through the SAME guard/allowlist/abort as 6.3. Test 'fires' cases against WEAK NPCs only.
tests.expected: Startup shows the reflex scancodes and a `(fast-PTT [<key>])` banner tag; under fire, tapping the reflex key + 'chaff!' deploys noticeably faster than the assistant path; when safe the same tap is refused (faster, not looser); synonyms map to chaff; 'abort' snap-releases held keys; a non-combat phrase falls through to a normal turn; the main PTT is untouched; clearing the reflex ptt disables the second hook.

steps.text: Startup readiness: launch reports the reflex scancodes on the `(PTT scan codes ... reflex ...)` line, and the banner's Reflexes line shows `(fast-PTT [<key>])`.

Instant fire under fire (defensive - safe): while a weak NPC is interdicting/shooting you, tap the reflex key and say 'chaff!' -> chaff deploys noticeably faster than the assistant path (no 'thinking'), log `phrase-spot fired chaff ...`.
Same guard when safe: parked/docked (not in danger), tap the reflex key and say 'chaff' -> refused ('you're not in combat...'), nothing pressed - the fast path is faster, not looser.
Synonyms: on the reflex key, 'flares' / 'break lock' also map to chaff (fire under fire to confirm; refused when safe).
Snap abort: on the reflex key, say 'abort' (or 'stop'/'release') -> releases every held key immediately (shared abort). Log: `aborted - released all keys`.
Falls through to a normal turn: on the reflex key, say a non-combat request ('what's my fuel level?') -> it is NOT treated as a reflex; it runs as an ordinary conversation turn and COVAS answers.
Main PTT untouched: the normal `[keys].push_to_talk` key still opens a normal conversation turn exactly as before; the two keys don't interfere.
Disabled by default: clear `[reflex].ptt` (blank) -> no second hook; only the main PTT works.

tests.title: LIVE-COMBAT: Tier-2 ambient auto-reflexes - fire on status threshold, no voice, cooldown, guard (issue #37)
tests.requires: SAFETY-CRITICAL / LIVE-COMBAT. These fire REAL keypresses with NO prompt - test the 'fires' cases against WEAK NPCs only. `[reflex].enabled = true` + `[reflex.auto].enabled = true`, then opt a reflex in: `[reflex.auto.heat_sink].enabled` and/or `[reflex.auto.chaff].enabled`. Keep `[reflex].combat_guard = true`. Bind DeployHeatSink and/or FireChaffLauncher to KEYS in ED. Requires `[elite].enabled`.
tests.expected: Startup shows `Reflexes : ON (auto ON)` and per-reflex bind lines; auto-chaff fires once under fire then holds for the cooldown; auto heat sink deploys on overheat in combat; the guard blocks a safe overheat (and combat_guard=false is the escape hatch); a disabled reflex stays quiet while the other still fires; 'abort' releases held keys; the master off stops all auto-fire (verbal 6.3 still works).

steps.text: Startup readiness: launch reports `Reflexes : ON (auto ON)` and, per enabled reflex, `Auto-reflex: heat_sink -> <key>` / `chaff -> <key>` (or `... UNUSABLE (no keyboard bind for DeployHeatSink/FireChaffLauncher)`).

Auto chaff under fire (defensive - safe): with auto-chaff on, let a weak NPC interdict you -> chaff fires automatically once shortly after the danger/interdiction begins. Log: `auto-chaff on EnteredDanger|Interdicted: Chaff away ...`.

Cooldown holds the repeat: stay in the fight past the danger onset -> it does NOT re-fire until the `[reflex.auto.chaff].cooldown` (default 8s) elapses. Log shows `chaff suppressed: chaff cooldown (8s)` for held attempts.
Auto heat sink on overheat (in combat): while in danger, push your ship over 100% heat -> a heat sink deploys automatically. Log: `auto-heat_sink on Overheating: Heat sink deployed ...`.

Guard blocks when safe: overheat while NOT in danger (flying too close to a star, parked) with `combat_guard = true` -> it refuses and nothing fires. Then set `[reflex].combat_guard = false`, repeat -> it DOES deploy on overheat regardless of danger (the escape hatch).
Disabled reflex stays quiet: turn `[reflex.auto.chaff].enabled = false` (leave heat_sink on) -> interdiction fires NO chaff; overheat still deploys a heat sink.
Hard abort: say 'abort' mid-reaction -> releases every held key (shared with keybinds/honk/verbal reflexes).
Master off: set `[reflex.auto].enabled = false` -> nothing auto-fires (verbal 6.3 still works if allowlisted).
